result:
I’d give every human mind a visceral, unshakable sense of how much every other mind suffers.

Not a polite intellectual knowledge, but the raw, embodied feeling—so that when you reach for the doorknob at the diner where your ex waits tables, her exhaustion blooms behind your eyes; when a refugee’s photo scrolls past, the salt taste of his terror sits on your tongue; when you vote, you carry the bruised lungs of the kid downwind of the power plant into the booth with you.

It would collapse the distance that lets us hoard, bomb, ignore, scroll on. Cruelty would become as impossible as forgetting you’re on fire. Compassion wouldn’t be a virtue; it would be gravity—inescapable, grounding, the thing that keeps us from drifting into the void alone.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
